resources.string: A shared space to cultivate our family dreams, track S.M.A.R.T. goals and strengthen our daily bonds.
Meals, groceries, and weekly planning for the family.
Invite people to collaborate on nutrition, adventures, and budget.
Manage people in %1$s — shared across nutrition, adventures, and budget.
Trips, outings, and experiences we plan together.
Spending, savings, and shared family finances.
Could not open nutrition. Try again.
Supabase is not configured on this build.
Weekly groceries, meals, and tailored advice for the family.
Build and check off this week's shopping list.
Plan lunch and dinner for each day of the week.
Ask specific questions about meals, allergies, and household habits.
Add an item for this week
No items yet. Add your first grocery item.
No new items to add (already on the AI list)
Could not generate groceries for this meal
e.g. High-protein lunches for two adults and one child
Could not generate advice. Try again.
That item is already on your list
Everything is checked off. Add more items or clear checked items below.
Describe goals, allergies, budget, or keywords (e.g. high protein, nut-free, quick dinners)
Meal plan preview for the full week. Review below, then apply.
Meal plan preview for %1$s. Review below, then apply.
Read-only list from the AI adviser. Your editable list is below.
Budget meal plan for the family
Write a sentence or keyword and the AI Architect will help you plan it.
Example: Buy a motorcycle, Trip to Spain...
Defining S.M.A.R.T. parameters for your success.
Establish solid bases and healthy habits
What are we trying to improve together?
Start with the couple's intent, choose the best category, then answer only the questions that matter for that category.
Example: Make weeknight dinners easier without wasting groceries
Review the AI structure below, edit anything that feels off, then save the plan as a category-specific Dream Card.
Why it matters for the household
Schedules, appointments, events, errands and coordination.
Cleaning, repairs, home routines, supplies and recurring upkeep.
Menus, grocery planning, cooking prep and kitchen inventory.
Shared budgets, bills, spending dynamics and financial goals.
Habits, appointments, movement, rest and personal care.
Big goals, milestones, plans and longer horizon projects.
Turns the meal plan into a focused shopping action.
Reduces weekday friction before the household gets busy.
Keeps the week's meals visible and realistic.
Clarifies the shared money agreement before bills are tracked.
Prevents repeated reminders and back-and-forth payments.
Creates one shared source of truth for household bills.
Helps the couple start from team language instead of blame.
Protects connection with a concrete, low-friction plan.
Creates the review loop that keeps the plan alive.
Suggested by the category AI plan.
Do this first to create momentum today.
Keeps the project realistic before decisions become expensive.
Names the main source of delay so the follow-up plan can remove it.
Clarifies ownership so the project does not depend on one person remembering everything.
Creates the recurring review loop for progress and accountability.
Breaks the milestone into visible, trackable project parts.
Coordinate a week, event, appointment rhythm, or errands without one partner carrying the calendar.
A shared logistics plan with owners, reminders, and the next schedule decision.
Answer the coordination questions so the AI can separate dates, owners, reminders, and dependencies.
Reset a household routine, maintenance backlog, cleaning rhythm, or supplies system.
A practical home operations plan with recurring routines and fair ownership.
Answer the home context questions so the AI can balance effort, cadence, and ownership.
Plan meals, groceries, prep blocks, or fridge-clearance ideas that fit real household constraints.
A weekly meal plan with grocery guidance, prep blocks, and realistic cooking constraints.
Answer the meal questions so the AI can respect allergies, time, portions, and local shopping context.
Reduce money stress, clarify bill ownership, split costs fairly, or create a shared spending rhythm.
A household finance blueprint with split rules, bill cadence, spending snapshot, and settle-up workflow.
Answer the finance questions so the AI can build a neutral ledger, not a generic budget.
Improve connection, translate conflict, plan care gestures, or protect energy as a couple.
A couples wellness plan with a de-escalation script, care action, radar, and next check-in.
Answer the wellness questions so the AI can coach tone, timing, and support without taking sides.
Move a big life milestone forward without overwhelm, unclear roles, or decision fatigue.
A milestone roadmap with decisions, owners, budget guardrails, and next best actions.
Answer the project questions so the AI can turn the milestone into a staged, trackable roadmap.
The beating heart of our family.
These models are designed to strengthen the psychology of consistency over perfection. Every small action builds our family's future.
You + Them vs. The Problem. Use this card to translate tension into a shared next step.
No plan steps yet. Add tasks below to start tracking this milestone.
All generated plan steps are complete. Add the next follow-up task when the milestone moves into a new phase.
Receipt text-to-split MVP: paste receipt text or enter the bill manually, then the app applies %1$s.
All logged variable bills are balanced
Paste receipt text or enter the fields manually. Camera OCR and email forwarding can feed this same parser later.
Paste receipt text, e.g. ENEL Energia 2026-05-16 Total $143.20
These values are derived from the monthly amounts reported during setup. Receipt import or manual bill entries can refine the history over time.
These answers define what the AI can safely suggest for recipes, portions and weekly prep.
How many people are we cooking for?
Scales ingredient quantities and portion sizes.
Are there any dietary restrictions or allergies?
Acts as a hard filter for restricted foods and allergens.
Are there any ingredients you absolutely hate?
Keeps meal plans away from foods the household dislikes.
These answers keep plans realistic for busy days and match the cooking effort you want.
On a busy weeknight, how much time do you realistically have to cook?
Filters recipes by prep and cook time.
What is your cooking style/skill level?
Keeps meal suggestions aligned with your preferred complexity.
How do you prefer to handle lunches?
Lets the AI decide whether to scale dinners for leftovers.
Use this when you open the app in a panic and need the AI to pick the right behavior mode immediately.
Sets the AI mode: batch planning, inventory clearance, or grocery logistics.
Allow GPS geolocation or provide your location manually so meal plans and grocery lists can be tailored with nearby shops, seasonal local products, and realistic availability.
How should we localize grocery suggestions?
This helps tailor grocery lists to local shops and products.
Add a city, neighborhood, postal code, or market area.
Generate weekly meal plan with AI
Complete each Meal Planning answer first so the AI can build a realistic weekly plan.
I prefer to cook lunches fresh/separately
🧊 Tell me what to make with what's in my fridge right now
🛒 Just generate a quick grocery list
These answers let the AI create a custom household ledger for bill tracking, smart splitting and one clean settle-up rhythm.
How do you prefer to split your shared household expenses?
This becomes the default math rule for each bill in the ledger.
What are the approximate partner incomes?
Optional in a real app, but useful here so the AI can calculate a fair-share split.
What fixed split should we use?
Example: 60/40, 70/30, or Partner A 60% / Partner B 40%.
How do you settle up right now?
Lets the AI decide whether to create smart settle, lump-sum previews or joint-account reminders.
Which recurring shared bills do you manage?
Select all that apply. These become starter ledger categories and calendar placeholders.
What is the biggest headache with bills right now?
This chooses the first automation the AI should emphasize.
Add the monthly amounts you already know. The ledger will use this to show a clear per-month spending breakdown while receipts and variable bills improve it over time.
How do you currently split your finances?
Determines whether the app should emphasize joint pools, separate tracking, or expense splitting.
Who currently manages the day-to-day household bills?
Identifies mental load and bill ownership.
What is your biggest daily financial annoyance right now?
Sets the first feature focus for the AI.
Scale: 1 = Strict Saver, 5 = Spontaneous Spender. Each partner should answer individually.
Helps detect saver-spender dynamics without blaming either partner.
Pairs with Partner A\'s answer so the AI can act as a neutral mediator.
How often do you talk about money together?
Shapes the tone and cadence of AI insights.
What is your primary financial goal for the next 12 months?
Gives the AI a North Star for budget and automation suggestions.
Do you have a plan for irregular expenses?
Shows whether the AI should recommend a sinking fund.
Complete the bill split, settle-up flow, recurring bill list and finance context first so the AI can build the household ledger.
Assign specific bills to specific people
We send Venmos/Zelles back and forth constantly
One person pays everything, and we calculate a lump sum at the end of the month
We have a shared joint account that we both fund, and bills auto-pay from there
Forgetting when a bill is due (Late fees)
The awkwardness of nagging my partner to send their half
Calculating who owes what when bills fluctuate (like electric/gas)
Feeling like the Project Manager who has to log into 10 different sites
A mix (joint account for bills, separate for personal)
We try to do it together
Saving for a big milestone (wedding, baby, house, vacation)
No, we just handle them as they hit our credit cards
Capture tiny answers that help the AI mediate, translate and plan for the team without taking sides.
De-escalator: what are you fighting about?
The AI rewrites blame into a calmer \'I feel / I need / can we\' message.
Paste what you want to say before sending it
Pick quick constraints so the AI can suggest a tiny appreciation action that actually lands.
What is draining you the most this week?
This gives the AI a prevention signal before the couple runs out of fuel.
Capture constraints so the AI can remove the \'I don\'t know, what do you want to do?\' loop.
We like sushi, cards, cooking, music...
We do not want to watch a movie
Golden rule: You + Them vs. The Problem. The AI should never judge who is right.
Answer four quick prompts and one sentence so the AI can build the project plan for you.
What big milestone are you tackling next?
This tells the AI what kind of project mountain it is planning around.
What\'s the biggest roadblock keeping you from finishing or starting this?
This changes whether the AI emphasizes steps, alignment, ownership or momentum.
What is your realistic timeline for this?
Sets the size and cadence of the micro-step checklist.
How would you describe your budget for this?
Helps the AI choose between DIY steps, vendor work and quote tracking.
In one sentence, what does a successful finish look like to you?
Example: A completely organized garage where we can actually park both cars by summer.
Example: Moving into our new house without losing our minds or overspending on movers.
Complete the milestone, roadblock, timeline, budget and success sentence so the AI can build the roadmap.
Big Event (Wedding, milestone party, baby shower)
Major life transition (New baby, career change, kids starting school)
Time: We are too busy with daily life to focus on it.
Alignment: We aren\'t on the same page about the budget, style, or plan.
Overwhelmed: We don\'t know the correct order of steps to take.
Accountability: We start, but things keep getting pushed to the back burner.
Moderate (Some DIY, hiring pros for the big stuff)
Full Service (We want to hire vendors/professionals)
Sign in to share family logistics with the people you choose.
Already have an account? Sign in
This sign-in option will be available soon.
Could not sign in. Check your details and try again.
Supabase is not configured on this build. Add supabase.anonKey to local.properties.
Enter a valid email and password.
Password must be at least 6 characters.
An account with this email already exists. Sign in instead.
Confirm your email before signing in.
New sign-ups are not available right now.
Account created. Check your email to confirm, then sign in.
Choose or create a space and pick who shares grocery, meal plan, and AI advice.
No sharing spaces yet. Create one to invite family or friends.
e.g. Home with Sara, Ski trip groceries
Could not load sharing spaces. Try again.
Enter a name for this space.
Select at least one shared feature.
Supabase is not configured on this build.
Invite people by email. Editors can change shared lists and plans; viewers can read them. This household applies to nutrition, adventures, and budget.
No members yet. Add someone to start sharing.
Could not update members. Try again.
No account found for that email yet.
Supabase is not configured on this build.
Shared sync offline — changes save locally
Pick an end date for trip or event groups.
Invite sent. They can join after signing up.
Only the household owner can invite or remove members.
